Nova Ljubljanska Banka announces robust 2024 financial results

By
Company News
Published 10/04/2025, 16:36
Nova Ljubljanska Banka announces robust 2024 financial results
View all comments (0)0

Nova Ljubljanska Banka announces robust 2024 financial results

LJUBLJANA - Nova Ljubljanska Banka d.d. (NLB) reported a strong financial performance for the year 2024, with a net income after tax reaching EUR 515 million, according to the NLB Group Annual Report 2024 released today. The bank has also declared a 100% increase in its dividend payment from the previous year's profit, totaling EUR 220 million.

NLB's growth strategy, unveiled in the past year, aims to double the Group's balance sheet to over EUR 50 billion in assets and achieve recurring revenues of more than EUR 2 billion and a profit of more than EUR 1 billion by 2030. The Group's recent acquisition of SLS Group and its re-entry into the Croatian market after 30 years are significant steps towards accelerating this growth plan.

Despite challenges from economic policies in the United States affecting the region, NLB remains optimistic about the potential for economic realignment in Europe to benefit its operations. However, the bank acknowledges that short-term market fluctuations and impacts on consumer confidence could temporarily dampen loan demand.

NLB's commitment to sustainability has been reinforced with an improved ESG Risk Rating of 10.5. The report also includes the inaugural Sustainability Statement under the new Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, highlighting the bank's dedication to sustainable practices.

The bank intends to propose a distribution of 50% of the 2024 profit, or EUR 257 million, for dividend payments in 2025, to be made in two tranches, with the first proposed at the regular General Meeting scheduled for June 2025. This proposal is contingent on no significant mergers and acquisitions activity occurring.

The NLB Group's performance and strategic milestones are detailed in the annual report, which has been confirmed by the NLB Supervisory Board. The report is available on the Financial Conduct Authority's National Storage Mechanism, the Ljubljana Stock Exchange website, and NLB's official website.

The information in this article is based on a press release statement by NLB.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.
